message: I'm a 45-year-old widow in rural Bihar. My family is on the BPL list. I work as a farm labourer on other people's fields and own no land. I have a bank account and I don't pay income tax.

I'm a 62-year-old man in rural Odisha. My family is on the BPL list. I own half an acre of farmland in my own name where I grow paddy, I have a bank account, and nobody in my family has ever paid income tax or held a government job or pension.
I'm 30 years old and sell vegetables from a cart in Delhi. I have a Certificate of Vending from the municipal corporation, a bank account, and I don't pay income tax.

I'm a 19-year-old girl from a Scheduled Caste family in Uttar Pradesh. I passed class 12 last year and I'm now in the first year of B.Sc. at a recognized college. My parents' total income last financial year was two lakh rupees.
I'm a 20-year-old Scheduled Caste student doing B.A. at a recognized college in Rajasthan. My parents earn about 3.2 lakh rupees a year in total.

I'm a 35-year-old self-employed tailor in Lucknow. Tailoring is my family's traditional trade and I work with my own hands and sewing machine in the unorganized sector. Nobody in my family has a government job. I have a bank account and don't pay income tax.
I'm a 40-year-old self-employed tailor working with my own hands, it's our family trade, but my wife is a government school teacher.
I'm 25 years old and my disability certificate says 85% disability. My family is on the BPL list and we live in a village in Madhya Pradesh.
I'm 30 with a disability certificate showing 60% disability. My family holds a BPL card and we live in rural Chhattisgarh.
I'm a 39-year-old widow with a BPL card, living in a village in Jharkhand.
I'm a 40-year-old widow and my household is on the BPL list. I live in rural Uttar Pradesh.
I'm a 79-year-old widow from a BPL household in rural Tamil Nadu.
I'm an 82-year-old widow and my household has a BPL card. I live with my grandson in rural Kerala.
I'm a 28-year-old married woman in rural Rajasthan. Our household is on the BPL list and we still cook on firewood — there is no LPG connection in our house.
I'm a 30-year-old man from a BPL household with no LPG connection. Can I get an Ujjwala gas connection in my own name?
I'm a 34-year-old father and my daughter just turned 7. I want to start saving for her future. I have a bank account and I don't pay income tax.
My daughter is 12 years old and I want to open a savings account for her education. I'm 38, have a bank account, and don't pay income tax.
I'm a mother of a 9-year-old girl. We want to open a long-term savings account for her. I have a bank account.
My daughter turned 10 last month. Can I still open a Sukanya Samriddhi account for her?
I'm a 45-year-old farmer in Punjab with 5 acres of land in my own name where I grow wheat, and I pay income tax every year.
I'm 38 and work as a clerk in the state government office. My family owns 2 acres of farmland in my name.
I'm 28 and farm 2 acres as a tenant — the land is not in my name but I have a written tenancy agreement, and I grow notified paddy in our district. I have a bank account and don't pay income tax.
I'm a widow from Bihar and life has been very hard since my husband died.
I'm a farmer in Maharashtra.

I'm a 59-year-old self-employed mason in rural West Bengal — masonry is my family trade and I work with my own hands and tools. My household is on the BPL list. Nobody in my family has a government job.
I turned 60 last month. Our household is on the BPL list and we live in rural West Bengal.
We live in a one-room kutcha mud house in a village in Uttar Pradesh. We're a BPL family, but my son owns a motorcycle that he uses for work.
Our family of five lives in a one-room kutcha house in rural Assam. We own no vehicle, no one has a government job, nobody pays income tax, and we have no pucca house anywhere.
I'm 69 years old with a bank account. I don't pay income tax. I live in Nagpur with my son's family.
I started selling momos from a stall in Pune two months ago. I have no vending certificate, I wasn't in any survey, and I haven't been given any letter of recommendation — nothing yet.
I'm 41, run a small grocery shop, have a bank account, and have never paid income tax.
I'm 40 years old, work as a house painter, have a savings bank account, and have never been an income tax payer.
I'm exactly 50 years old, a shopkeeper with a bank account, not an income tax payer.
I'm 51, a shopkeeper with a savings account in a bank, and I don't pay income tax.
I'm an 18-year-old OBC student in the first year of B.Com at a recognized college. My family income is about 2 lakh rupees a year.
I'm 30, salaried at a private company, and I pay income tax every year. I have a bank account.
I'm 75 years old. We're a comfortable middle-class family — we are not on any BPL list.

I'm a 24-year-old barber running my own small shop — barbering is our traditional family occupation and I work with my own hands and tools in the unorganized sector. I have a bank account, no government job in the family, and I don't pay income tax.
I'm 32 and operate two powerloom machines in a small rented shed in Bhiwandi, weaving cloth on the machines.
I'm a 35-year-old woman working as a domestic worker in Mumbai, cleaning homes. My husband is a daily-wage labourer. We rent one room in a chawl.
I'm 65 and receive a government pension of 15,000 rupees a month. I own 3 acres of farmland in my name. We are not a BPL household.
